front.title: RDAP Extension for DNS Time-To-Live (TTL Values)

abstract.t: This document specifies an extension to the Registration Data Access Protocol which allows the Time-To-Live (TTL) values for relevant DNS record types to be included in RDAP responses.

note.title: About this draft
note.t: The source for this draft, and an issue tracker, may can be found at .

section.name: Introduction
section.t: While allows RDAP server operators to provide information about the content of the NS, DS, A and AAAA RRset(s) (see ) which are published in the DNS for a given registry object (domain or host object), it does not provide a mechanism to allow the Time-To-Live (TTL) (see ) values of those RRsets to be included in responses. Inclusion of these values in RDAP responses (in addition to nameservers, glue IP addresses, and DS records) allows out-of-band debugging of the DNS configuration of troublesome domain names.
This document describes how TTL information can be included in domain and nameserver objects in RDAP responses. As per , TTL values are applicable to RRSets rather than individual records.

section.name: Conventions used in this document
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.
This document makes use of the terms defined in .

section.name: RDAP Response Specification
section.t: Servers that support this extension MAY include a "ttl0_data" property in any domain () and nameserver () objects included in RDAP responses. As per , clients which do not implement this specification SHOULD ignore the "ttl0_data" member.
The ttl0_data property is an object which has the following properties:

ul.li: A "values" property, which is an object which maps DNS record type mnemonics to TTL values;
An OPTIONAL "remarks" property, which is an array of remarks (see ).

section.t: As specified in , a TTL value is a "an unsigned number, with a minimum value of 0, and a maximum value of 2147483647. That is, a maximum of 2^31 - 1.". TTL values are represented as JSON numbers.
An example domain object with valid "ttl0_data" attribute is provided below. Readers should refer to for a description of the other objects listed in the example.
An example nameserver object with valid "ttl0_data" attribute is provided below.

section.name: DNS record Types and TTL Values
section.t: The DNS record type mnemonics that appear as the property names in values objects MUST be in all capitals and MUST be registered with IANA in . TTL values MUST be unsigned integers in the range 0-2147483647 as per .

section.name: RDAP Conformance
section.t: Servers returning responses containing TTL values MUST include the string "ttl0" in the rdapConformance array.

section.name: RDAP Servers
section.t: This specification is complementary to the Extensible Provisioning Protocol (EPP) Mapping for DNS Time-to-Live (TTL) Values, but registry operators do not need to implement that extension in their EPP server in order to implement this RDAP extension.

section.name: RDAP Clients
section.t: Many RDAP clients make use of frameworks which automatically "hydrate" objects using JSON data received in RDAP responses. As a result, RDAP clients which use these frameworks may SHOULD explicitly carve out the "values" property of "ttl0_data" elements.

Since the list of record types appearing in "ttl0_data" elements may change with time, clients which implement this extension MUST accept responses containing values for all valid DNS record types, and SHOULD periodically update the list of valid DNS record types to align with .

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: IANA is requested to register the following value in the RDAP Extensions Registry:
Registry operator: Any
Published specification: this document
Contact: IETF <iesg@ietf.org>
Intended usage: this extension describes how DNS TTL values can be included in RDAP responses.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: Security services for the extension specified in this document are described in .

This document only concerns itself with the representation of configured TTL values for domain and host objects. Such configuration is out of scope. Readers may refer to for the security implications of configuring those values.

section.name: ICANN RDAP
section.t: Organization: ICANN
Name: ICANN RDAP
Description: ICANN RDAP contains an RDAP client, server, and common libraries.
Level of maturity: Mature
Coverage: All aspects of the protocol are implemented.
Licensing: Apache License, Version 2.0 or MIT License.
Contact: globalsupport@icann.org
URL: https://github.com/icann/icann-rdap

section.name: Net::RDAP
section.t: Name: Net::RDAP
Description: An RDAP library for Perl.
Level of maturity: Mature
Coverage: All aspects of the protocol are implemented.
Licensing: Perl (Artistic License or GPL).
Contact: gavin.brown@fastmail.uk
URL: https://metacpan.org/pod/Net::RDAP

section.name: rdapper
section.t: Name: rdapper
Description: A command-line RDAP client that uses Net::RDAP.
Level of maturity: Mature
Coverage: All aspects of the protocol are implemented.
Licensing: Perl (Artistic License or GPL).
Contact: gavin.brown@fastmail.uk
URL: https://metacpan.org/pod/App::rdapper

section.name: Changes from 08 to 09
section.t: Updates as per directorate review:

ol.li: Improved wording in the last sentence of the first paragraph of .
Add SHOULD keyword in to clarify the need to carve out the "values" property of "ttl0_data" elements.
Updated .

section.name: Changes from 07 to 08
section.t: Updates as per AD review:

ol.li: Added .
Move the last paragraph of to .
Remove most of , moved some of it to .
Backfilled the change log immediately below.
Correct reference to .
Add missing change log entries to assist in IESG review.
Added .
Clarify that TTL values are represented using JSON numbers.

section.name: Changes from 03 to 04
section.t: Updates based on feedback during WGLC:

ol.li: Remove the xref in the Abstract to avoid issues when rendered as plaintext;
Refer to RRsets instead of resource records since requires the TTL of all the records in an RRset to be the same;
Reference which defines what a TTL is.
Clarify that clients need to handle (even if to ignore) unexpected record types.
Switch to array model to object model, based on WG feedback.
Added .
Include reference to in (thanks Jasdip Singh).
Removed the value member of the link object in the example domain object so that it conforms with web linking practice (also thanks Jasdip Singh).
Updated the extension identifier and extension property name to align with the current best practices in .
Added .
Changed MUST to MAY in the first paragraph of .
Reduce ambiguity around the repetition of DNS record types in responses.
Name change only.
Update reference to .
Update reference to the EPP extension.
Extension property name renamed to ttl.
The extension data structure is now an array allowing common TTL values, remarks and events to be mapped to multiple DNS record types.
The extension data structure may now include remarks and events.
Added normative text regarding the value of DNS record mnemonics and TTL values.

section.name: Acknowledgements
section.t: The author wishes to thank the following for their constructive feedback and advice during the development of this document:
Andy Newton, Pawel Kowalik, Maarten Wullink, Mohamed Boucadair, Vijay K. Gurbani, Di Ma, Nabeel Cocker.
